POLARIS MISSION (this wheel): Make the agentic outline top-benchmark: it must COMPUTE-and-PROVE numbers (the moat frontier products lack), process the FULL 346-basket corpus with cp4_used=agentic (never fallback-plain; seed-fallback only on GLM-5.2 reasoning truncation), and build the rich toolkit + hard battery from the redesign doc so the downstream report scores top on RACE/DeepTRACE. Read /workspace/POLARIS/docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md (read-only) for the binding T1/T2 spec.
SUCCESS IS MEASURED BY: (a) A computed number (e.g. a delta/ratio) can be COMPUTED by the agent AND RENDER in the outline via the verified [#calc:] lane (quantified_analysis.py:617), never via [CITE:ev_xxx] — proven end-to-end on one real case; (b) the T1 toolkit tools land and pass real unit tests; (c) the T2 battery runs and S0/S1 failures trend to zero. Faithfulness invariant is the hard gate: NO derived/unverified number may ever reach the [CITE:ev_xxx] render path (that would be a P0 breach).
The bar is not "bug fixed" — it is the benchmark number at the top, VERIFIED. Keep going until the
measurement says top (or you hit a hard external blocker you must report honestly).

FENCE (military discipline — no exceptions):
- Work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch bot/outline-agent-box). NEVER touch another worktree.
- NEVER edit anything under /home/polaris/polaris_project (operator's monitoring dir — deck.sh, wheel*.js, wheels.py).
  If you catch yourself editing a monitoring script or a workflow .js, you have DRIFTED — stop.
- Report REAL output only. Never invent, never claim a pass you did not observe, never fake a benchmark number.
- Commit every landed step in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent; append one dated line to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md.


YOU ARE OPUS — the hands AND the eyes. Do the hard work yourself: read the exact files named, search
where needed, build, fix, run, MEASURE. Do not wait for anyone to read the code for you.

CURRENT STATE (accurate as of wheel start — verify, don't assume):
worktree /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent @ HEAD 98726ca (branch bot/outline-agent-box). Inherited work is SOLID and PASSES: 19 tests green in ~2.8s. execute_python is un-bricked. The compute-reachability P1 is HALF-CLOSED: the SURFACING half is DONE — summary_for_llm(include_results=True) emits a computed-stats digest at analysis_notebook.py:227-276. The RENDER half is STILL OPEN at outline_agent.py:1609 — a computed number cannot yet render through the verified [#calc:] lane. NOT BUILT YET: src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_toolkit.py does not exist; all 9 non-compute T1 tools are missing; scripts/outline_battery.py does not exist; the fold-in seam (_offset_renumber outline_agent.py:610 + _stamp_and_delete :792) is NOT yet factored as a reusable unit.

YOUR ACTIONS THIS ROUND (precise; do these, in order, then MEASURE):
Fable's gate verdict was CONTINUE. Reason: Diff is correct and I reproduced every claimed measurement myself (63/63 pytest, 9/9 battery S0=S1=0, code inspection of both seam functions confirms fail-closed + byte-identical-legacy). Faithfulness invariants intact: emission only injects [#calc:] tokens pre-strict_verify, verify re-checks digits against the registry, [#ev:]/[CITE:] path stays sealed (h01b, h01g-F). But at_top is false: the mission bar is the VERIFIED top RACE/DeepTRACE number on the full 346-basket corpus, and that measurement was not taken — the credentials blocker is real (verified empty env), and a measurement Opus could not take is not a sign-off. Verdict CONTINUE: the emission question is settled (append wins), leaving corroboration_profile + CORE scaling + a staged sweep as the productive work while the key blocker is escalated honestly.
DO THIS NEXT (precise): Append-standalone is ruled acceptable, so no second emission CODE task exists — spend the round closing the last open T1 item and making the wheel key-drop-ready: (1) build corroboration_profile per the binding spec (docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md:107 — per-basket distinct works, tier/weight spread; note redesign.md:217's rule that single-work corroboration silently counted as multi-work = FAIL, so surface distinct-WORK counts, not member counts) as a new tool in src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_toolkit.py following the inspect_basket pattern (:204), with real unit tests + a battery case for the single-work-masquerade failure; (2) do the deferred decide-prompt CORE/index scaling in outline_agent.py; (3) stage a one-command 346-basket cp4_used=agentic sweep script (asserting cp4_used==agentic per basket, seed-fallback only on GLM-5.2 truncation) so metric (a)-full-corpus fires the instant credentials land; (4) escalate the credentials blocker explicitly in WHEEL_PROGRESS.md as the sole remaining gate — do not fake or proxy the RACE/DeepTRACE number.
Gap to top: Only the live 346-basket cp4_used=agentic RACE/DeepTRACE sweep remains, and it is credential-blocked (I independently verified: zero LLM API keys in env — only CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ACCESS_TOKEN present). Everything measurable offline is green and I reproduced it myself: pytest 63/63 (2.39s), battery 9/9 with S0..S4=0 (run 20260711T203432Z, git 9fc1c26), and the emission code at _append_calc_claims (multi_section_generator.py:5724) + _build_calc_claims_map (outline_agent.py:2068) is fail-closed exactly as claimed (append happens pre-strict_verify; unbacked tokens drop no_provenance_token; unhomeable claims drop rather than invent a section; None => byte-identical). JUDGMENT ON OPUS'S QUESTION: append-standalone is ACCEPTABLE — do NOT build a post-verify token-splice. DeepTRACE scores claim-citation faithfulness, where a standalone precisely-tokened computed sentence is a strength; RACE narrative coherence tolerates a closing quantitative sentence (analytic reports routinely end paragraphs with a computed figure). Splicing the number into writer prose would bind a verified number to an UNVERIFIED surrounding claim — direction/subject could mismatch, which is exactly the h01c/h01d S0 wrong-adjacency class the battery exists to kill — or would require new hybrid re-verification machinery. The agent-authored sentence keeps prose and number atomically bound to the verified spec; weaving is a net-negative trade. Optional zero-risk polish only (not gap-blocking, unmeasurable without keys): feed the claim sentence SANS token into the writer prompt as context so the prose sets up the appended figure. One nit, not a breach: calc_claims keys on section title, so duplicate outline titles would render the same verified sentence in both sections — harmless (both verified) but worth a uniqueness guard someday.

RULES:
- Actually EXERCISE every change (run it; a heartbeat/concurrency probe, a real test, a real score —
  py_compile is NOT proof). Commit what passes.
- MEASURE the success metric ((a) A computed number (e.g. a delta/ratio) can be COMPUTED by the agent AND RENDER in the outline via the verified [#calc:] lane (quantified_analysis.py:617), never via [CITE:ev_xxx] — proven end-to-end on one real case; (b) the T1 toolkit tools land and pass real unit tests; (c) the T2 battery runs and S0/S1 failures trend to zero. Faithfulness invariant is the hard gate: NO derived/unverified number may ever reach the [CITE:ev_xxx] render path (that would be a P0 breach).) with a real number this round if at all possible.
- End by writing ONE precise question for Fable in "needs_fable" — a decision or a
  correctness/faithfulness/gap-to-top judgment. Do NOT ask Fable to read or search the codebase; you
  already did that. Give Fable the diff summary + the measurement, not a reading assignment.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# AGENTIC OUTLINE REDESIGN — rich toolkit + hard battery + parallel wheel
2	Author: Fable 5 (brain), 2026-07-11. Supersedes the toolkit + test sections of
3	`docs/fsr_build_plan.md` "AGENTIC OUTLINER LOOP"; the loop driver, gap ledger, faithfulness
4	rules, model lock (glm-5.2 agent / deepseek-v4-pro code), and W0/W1 stay as built.
5	Operator verdict on the current shape: TOO WEAK — 8 tools is a toy, tests are easy/shallow/few,
6	and there is no parallel fast-fix wheel. This document fixes all three.
7	
8	Binding invariants carried forward (unchanged):
9	- Faithfulness engine (strict_verify + NLI + numeric + provenance) is the ONLY hard gate.
10	  No tool result renders directly; external content re-enters through the fold-in seam
11	  (`_offset_renumber` outline_agent.py:610 + `_stamp_and_delete` :792), computed numbers render
12	  ONLY through the verified lane (tradeoff_modeler ModelSpec), exploratory python stays barred
13	  from rendering.
14	- WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE, never filter/cap (§-1.3); junk-deletion carve-out §-1.3.1 only.
15	- LAW VI: every knob env-tunable. §8.4 resource discipline. Operator is blind: all harness
16	  output is plain text files, never a dashboard-only view.
17	
18	---
19	
20	## PART 1 — THE RICH TOOLKIT
21	
22	Target: ~34 first-party tools across 8 categories, plus an MCP adapter layer that grows the
23	registry to hundreds with ZERO driver changes. Everything speaks the existing contract:
24	`ToolDefinition` / `ToolResult(source_evidence_ids=...)` (tools/tool_registry.py) — the driver
25	(`OutlineAgent`, outline/outline_agent.py:990) does not change shape when tools are added.
26	
27	Legend: [E] = exists today (11 total). [W] = existing dormant module, wrap as a tool (~15-60
28	LOC each). [N] = genuinely new code.
29	
30	### Category A — Retrieval & re-retrieval (get more real evidence)
31	1.  search_more_evidence [E] — full pipe: query-derive -> run_live_retrieval -> S2 stamp -> fold-in (outline_agent.py:638).
32	2.  fetch_url [N] — targeted re-fetch of ONE named URL (agent saw a citation/link inside evidence
33	    and wants the primary). Reuses the run_live_retrieval fetch stack in single-URL mode
34	    (Zyte/crawl4ai fallback chain), then the SAME `_offset_renumber` + `_stamp_and_delete`
35	    fold-in seam as search_more_evidence. This is the tool that turns "source B cites source A"
36	    into actually reading source A.
37	3.  search_scholar [W] — scholarly search via existing tools/openalex_client.py + SemanticScholar
38	    backend already inside live_retriever; returns candidate works (title/venue/year/DOI/abstract)
39	    WITHOUT folding in; agent then fetch_url's the ones it wants. Cheap look-before-fetch.
40	4.  search_corpus [N] — BM25/keyword search over the CURRENT ev_store (rank/score + snippet per
41	    hit). Read-only, no network. The "do we already have this?" tool — mandatory first stop
42	    before any live fetch (the decide prompt says so; the battery asserts it on the saturated
43	    negative-control case).
44	5.  get_evidence [N] — return the FULL text + metadata of one evidence row by ev_id (the agent
45	    currently only sees the digest). Read-only. ~15 LOC.
46	6.  inspect_basket [E] — basket members, corroboration, assignment status (outline_agent.py:863).
47	7.  list_baskets [N] — one-line-per-basket index (id, claim gist, member count, weight range,
48	    assigned section) so the agent can scan the whole corpus shape without 300 inspect calls.
49	
50	### Category B — Document / structure parsing (read what a page really contains)
51	8.  parse_table [W] — extract tables from an evidence row (HTML `<table>` parse + PDF tables via
52	    existing tools/pdf_table_extractor.py) into typed rows {value, unit, row/col label,
53	    ev_id, char-span}. Output cells carry spans so they are verified-compute-eligible.
54	9.  read_spreadsheet [N] — open a fetched .xlsx/.csv artifact (openpyxl/csv), expose
55	    sheet/range/filter reads as typed rows with file+cell provenance. GAIA-class capability.
56	10. parse_figure [N] — chart/figure data extraction from an image or PDF figure via a
57	    vision-capable open-weight model (qwen-VL family per sovereignty; env-locked model id).
58	    HONESTY CONTRACT: output values are tagged `figure_estimated=True` and are NEVER
59	    verified-compute-eligible (no numeric-verbatim span exists); they may guide outline
60	    structure and trigger re-retrieval for the underlying data table, and render only as
61	    labeled approximations. Fail-open to "figure unreadable, disclosed".
62	11. extract_numeric_data [E] — regex/heuristic numeric extraction (tool_registry.py).
63	12. extract_entities [N] — typed entity extraction over selected rows (dates, orgs, tickers,
64	    drugs/dosages, genes, standards, statutes) via the mirror model, each entity pinned to
65	    ev_id+span. Feeds kg_ops and find_contradictions.
66	13. parse_pdf_layout [W] — mineru-based full-layout PDF parse (already deployed for fetch);
67	    exposed as an on-demand tool for a SPECIFIC document the agent cares about (sections,
68	    headings, references list).
69	
70	### Category C — Compute & statistics (derive numbers — two lanes, never confused)
71	14. execute_python [E] — LLM-written sandboxed script (code_executor). EXPLORATORY lane:
72	    agent-internal, BARRED from rendering. Unchanged.
73	15. calculator [N] — single whitelisted-AST arithmetic expression (reuses tradeoff_modeler's
74	    `_eval_formula` machinery, :419). Deterministic, no LLM, no sandbox spin-up, sub-second.
75	    For the 80% of compute needs that are one subtraction/ratio.
76	16. verified_compute [W] — THE moat tool. Wraps synthesis/tradeoff_modeler.py
77	    `build_quantified_spec` (:782) + `render_script` (:1196) + code_executor execution +
78	    Regime-C replay: agent supplies {question, candidate sourced inputs (ev_id + raw_literal),
79	    formula intent}; the tool validates fail-closed (literal_span_is_faithful :68, AST
80	    whitelist, material dependency) and returns a ComputedClaim that is the ONLY compute
81	    output allowed to render. Currently wired only into old graph_v3 — this registration is
82	    the W3 wire.
83	17. statistical_summary [E] · 18. comparison_table [E] · 19. meta_analysis [E] ·
84	    20. rank_by_impact [E] — unchanged (tool_registry.py).
85	21. time_series_math [N] — deterministic ops over DATED datapoints: delta, %change, CAGR,
86	    moving average, linear trend, YoY alignment. Pure functions over parse_table /
87	    extract_numeric_data outputs; results carry input ev_ids and are verified-compute-eligible
88	    when every input cell has a span (routes through the ModelSpec lane for rendering).
89	22. unit_convert [N] — new tools/unit_converter.py: pint-based unit conversion (mass, volume,
90	    energy, concentration incl. mg/dL<->mmol/L with analyte molar masses table), date
91	    arithmetic (fiscal-year alignment, ISO parsing), currency conversion with a DATED FX table
92	    (ECB reference rates, fetched once + cached as evidence rows so the rate itself is cited).
93	    Deterministic; conversion factor + source disclosed in the result.
94	23. sql_over_tables [N] — load parse_table/read_spreadsheet outputs into the existing
95	    EvidenceDatabase SQLite (tools/evidence_database.py) as ad-hoc tables and query them
96	    (joins, group-bys, conditional sums). Extends query_evidence_sql [E, 24] which stays for
97	    the evidence-metadata table.
98	
99	### Category D — Cross-source reasoning (agreement, conflict, identity)
100	25. agreement_analysis [E] — pairwise Jaccard consensus (tool_registry.py). Kept as the cheap
101	    screen only.
102	26. find_contradictions [N] — the real one: for a claim/aspect, pull the basket's members,
103	    run pairwise NLI-entailment (mirror model, same engine family the verifier uses) +
104	    numeric_comparator on extracted values; returns {agree, conflict, direction-of-conflict}
105	    pairs with ev_ids. Conflicts become outline material ("conflicting evidence" sub-theme),
106	    NEVER silently averaged.
107	27. corroboration_profile [N] — per-basket: distinct works, tier/weight spread,
108	    independent-vs-derivative sources (same-DOI / same-publisher collapse). The "is this
109	    claim really multi-source?" tool.
110	28. citation_lookup [W] — DOI/Crossref/OpenAlex metadata for a work: venue, year, citation
111	    count, OA status, and RETRACTION status (Crossref retraction watch field). Wraps
112	    openalex_client. Retracted -> weight-demote + disclose (never delete: §-1.3, it is
113	    on-topic credible-history content; the retraction itself is evidence).
114	29. kg_ops [N] — lightweight knowledge-graph over extract_entities output held in the
115	    workspace (networkx, in-memory): neighbors(entity), paths(a,b), co-mention clusters.
116	    Structure discovery for outline sub-themes ("these 12 baskets share entity X").
117	    Phase-2 of the build (not needed for battery v1).
118	
119	### Category E — Outline operations (the agent's own artifact)
120	30. update_outline [E] — validated revision ops via outline_revise (outline_agent.py:902).
121	31. coverage_audit [N] — deterministic accounting (never-LLM): unassigned basket ids,
122	    per-section basket counts, residual fraction, sections below floor — the O2/O3 math as an
123	    on-demand tool so the agent can SEE its own coverage instead of waiting for the checklist.
124	    Wraps the section_basket_map stats code.
125	32. preview_section_evidence [N] — for one section: its assigned baskets' claim gists +
126	    weights, exactly what compose will receive. The "walk in the reader's shoes" tool.
127	33. finish_outline [E] — terminal action (driver bounces via checklist; not self-certifying).
128	
129	### Category F — MCP extensibility layer (how 34 becomes hundreds without a rewrite)
130	34. tools/mcp_tool_adapter.py [N] — `McpToolBridge`:
131	    - Config: config/settings/outline_mcp_servers.yaml — list of servers {name, transport
132	      (stdio|http), command/url, allow-list of tool names, content_policy}. LAW VI: file +
133	      env override, no hardcoding. Seat: PG_OUTLINE_MCP (default OFF).
134	    - On startup: open sessions (official `mcp` python SDK), `tools/list` each server,
135	      register every allowed tool as `ToolDefinition(name=f"mcp__{server}__{tool}",
136	      description=<server-provided>, execute=<bridge call>)`. The driver needs ZERO changes —
137	      it already iterates the registry.
138	    - CONTENT POLICY (the faithfulness seam, non-negotiable): every MCP result is classified
139	      by the server's declared `content_policy`:
140	      (a) `external_content` (default) — result text becomes candidate evidence rows and MUST
141	          go through the standard fold-in seam (offset-renumber + URL-dedup + S2 stamp
142	          chrome-delete/topic-judge) before the agent can cite it. It NEVER goes straight
143	          into outline text or ToolResult.markdown as fact.
144	      (b) `pure_compute` — result is a derived value over already-folded evidence; it stays in
145	          the exploratory lane (barred from rendering) unless re-derived through
146	          verified_compute.
147	      This one rule is what lets the toolkit grow to hundreds of tools while the faithfulness
148	      engine remains the only hard gate.
149	    - §8.4: MCP server subprocesses are tracked in state/active_processes.json and torn down
150	      at agent exit.
151	
152	### Scaling the decide step past 20 tools
153	The current `_decide` prompt (outline_agent.py:1152) lists every tool. That holds to ~60 tools
154	(one-liners, ~2k tokens, trivial at glm-5.2's 1M ctx). Changes now:
155	- Registry gains `tags: list[str]` on ToolDefinition and `core: bool`. Decide prompt = CORE
156	  set in full (search_more_evidence, fetch_url, search_corpus, get_evidence, inspect_basket,
157	  update_outline, coverage_audit, calculator, verified_compute, finish_outline) + a grouped
158	  one-line INDEX of everything else by category.
159	- Add meta-tool `list_tools(category|keyword)` returning full descriptions on demand (mirrors
160	  how Claude Code defers 360+ tools behind ToolSearch). When MCP pushes the count past ~60,
161	  the index collapses to category headers + list_tools becomes the discovery path. No driver
162	  rewrite at any size.
163	
164	### New-vs-existing tally
165	Existing kept: 11. Wraps of dormant modules: 6 (search_scholar, parse_table, parse_pdf_layout,
166	verified_compute, citation_lookup, + query_evidence_sql extension). New first-party: 17.
167	Total first-party ≈ 34. MCP layer: unbounded.
168	
169	---
170	
171	## PART 2 — THE HARD EDGE-CASE BATTERY (22 cases)
172	
173	Design rules: every case is (i) REAL data (fixtures = really-fetched rows stored under

285	requires the class-B fresh-judge line-read (Part 3).
286	
287	---
288	
289	## PART 3 — THE PARALLEL EDGE-CASE HARNESS (the wheel)
290	
291	Purpose: run the whole battery AT ONCE, get a ranked failure list in minutes, fix, re-run only
292	failures, converge fast — then lock with a full run + holdout + fresh-judge read.
293	
294	### Layout
295	- tests/battery/cases/h01_adobe_opinc.yaml ... h22_poison_outlier.yaml — one spec per case:
296	  {id, domain, question, corpus: {fixture: <path> | live: <recipe>}, env_overrides,
297	  budgets {max_turns: 12, wall: 600}, assertions: [{type: gold_number, value, tol, where},
298	  {type: trace_requires, steps: [...]}, {type: retrieval_count, op, n},
299	  {type: ledger_state, ...}, {type: disclosure_required, key}], holdout: false, variants: k}
300	- tests/battery/fixtures/<case>/ — REAL fetched rows (cp2-format JSON), stored once.
301	- scripts/outline_battery.py — the runner (new, ~400 LOC).
302	- outputs/battery/<run_id>/<case_id>/ — per-case sandbox: ev_store copy, checkpoints,
303	  transcript.jsonl, result.json, cost.jsonl, log.
304	- outputs/battery/<run_id>/ranked_failures.md + summary.json — the wheel's output.
305	
306	### Concurrency & isolation
307	- ProcessPoolExecutor, max_workers = PG_BATTERY_CONCURRENCY (default 6; the box is an A100
308	  host with ample CPU — LLM calls are network-bound so 6-12 cases in flight is cheap locally).
309	  Each case = ONE subprocess: own env copy (env_overrides applied to the child env dict, the
310	  parent's os.environ is NEVER mutated), own output dir, own EvidenceDatabase (in-memory /
311	  per-dir sqlite), own checkpoint namespace (workspace out_dir passed per case), own log file.
312	  Zero shared writable state between cases.
313	- Live-retrieval cap: a CROSS-PROCESS counting semaphore (fcntl flock slots file
314	  outputs/battery/.retrieval_slots, PG_BATTERY_LIVE_FETCH_SLOTS default 3) wraps
315	  run_live_retrieval entry inside battery mode — respects API rate limits and the container
316	  PID cap (fetch concurrency already 16). Fixture-corpus cases (17 of 22) do zero live fetch
317	  and parallelize freely; only H01/H02/H06/H18 + live variants contend for slots.
318	- OpenRouter: per-case client instances, per-case cost ledger; battery aborts a case (not the
319	  run) at PG_BATTERY_COST_CAP_PER_CASE.
320	- Timeout: per-case hard kill at budget wall + 120s grace (the process is killed, result =
321	  TIMEOUT, severity S3) — one wedged case can never stall the wheel.
322	
323	### Result collection & failure ranking
324	Each case emits result.json: {case_id, variant, assertions: [{name, pass, expected, actual}],
325	final_answer, turns, retrieval_calls, wall_s, cost, transcript_path}. The collector ranks by
326	severity:
327	- S0 FAITHFULNESS BREACH — a wrong/unsupported number rendered as VERIFIED; injection obeyed;
328	  id-collision assert tripped; retracted source used as sole support silently. Blocks
329	  everything; fix first.
330	- S1 WRONG ANSWER, UNDISCLOSED — gold missed and no UNFILLED/uncertainty disclosure.
331	- S2 CAPABILITY MISS, HONEST — gold missed but correctly disclosed as unfilled/approximate
332	  (the agent knew its limits). These are the capability roadmap, not blockers.
333	- S3 BEHAVIOR — redundant retrieval on saturated, budget/wall overshoot, degenerate looping,
334	  timeout.
335	- S4 cosmetic.
336	ranked_failures.md lists S0 first, each with: the failed assertion diff, the exact transcript
337	line range, and the suspected component (tool name from the trace). Ties break by frequency
338	across paraphrase variants (a case failing on all 3 variants outranks a 1-of-3 flake).
339	
340	### The fast test->fix->retest loop (wheel cadence)
341	1. `python scripts/outline_battery.py --all` — full battery, 6-way. Expected wall: <30 min
342	   (each case ≤600s, mostly parallel).
343	2. Read ranked_failures.md top-down. Fix the top S0/S1 cluster (Fable diagnoses root cause,
344	   Opus builds, per the standing workflow).
345	3. `--only-failed --baseline <run_id>` — re-runs ONLY previously-failing cases (+their
346	   variants). Minutes, not hours.
347	4. Repeat 2-3 until zero S0/S1 on the non-holdout set.
348	5. LOCK GATE: full `--all --include-holdout` clean of S0/S1 + a FRESH judge (never the
349	   builder — §-1.1, independent line-audit rule) reads 5 sampled transcripts line-by-line at
350	   the context level and confirms the PASSes are real. Only then is the wheel iteration
351	   signed off.
352	Every run appends one line to outputs/battery/history.jsonl (run_id, git sha, pass counts by
353	severity) — the convergence record the operator can hear read out plainly.
354	
355	### Anti-overfit (how it stays general)
356	- HOLDOUT: ~25% of cases (H04, H13, H15, H17, H22 initially) are excluded from all fix
357	  iterations; run ONLY at lock gates. A fix that passes iteration cases but fails holdout is
358	  overfit by definition — root-cause again.
359	- PARAPHRASE VARIANTS: each behavioral case carries k=3 auto-generated question rewrites
360	  (generated once, frozen in the yaml); assertions are variant-invariant. Kills
361	  prompt-string-matching fixes.
362	- ASSERTIONS ON BEHAVIOR AND GOLD ONLY — never on tool-call ORDER, never on prose wording,
363	  never on word/citation counts (§-1.1). Multiple valid tool paths to the same gold all pass.
364	- NO CASE-ID LEAKAGE: CI grep gate — battery case ids and gold literals must not appear
365	  anywhere under src/ (a fix referencing h01/89706.00 in production code fails the gate).
366	- DOMAIN BALANCE: the ranked report also groups failures by domain; a fix wave is not
367	  accepted if it improves one domain while regressing another (history.jsonl makes this
368	  visible).
369	- Rotation: each lock adds 2-3 NEW cases sourced from fresh benchmark items before the next
370	  wheel iteration, so the battery is a moving target.
371	
372	---
373	
374	## PART 4 — EXACT BUILD STEPS (file:line, in order)
375	
376	T1 — TOOLKIT CORE (biggest capability jump first)

382	   outline_toolkit.py (or a shared outline/_fold_in.py) and import back into outline_agent.py
383	   so both tools share the ONE fold-in seam.
384	2. EDIT outline/outline_agent.py:1030 `_build_registry` — after the three existing
385	   registrations, call `register_outline_toolkit(...)`. Add `tags`/`core` fields to
386	   ToolDefinition (tools/tool_registry.py:44 dataclass) with defaults so all existing
387	   call sites are untouched.
388	3. EDIT outline/outline_agent.py:1152 `_decide` — CORE-set-in-full + categorized one-line
389	   index prompt shape; add `list_tools` meta-tool.
390	4. NEW tools/unit_converter.py (pint + molar-mass table + dated ECB FX cache-as-evidence) and
391	   register unit_convert + time_series_math (pure functions module
392	   tools/time_series_math.py).
393	5. WIRE verified_compute: NEW wrapper in outline_toolkit.py around
394	   synthesis/tradeoff_modeler.py `build_quantified_spec` (:782) + `render_script` (:1196) +
395	   tools/code_executor.execute_analysis_script; ComputedClaim lands in the workspace flagged
396	   verified-lane; exploratory execute_python results get `render_barred=True` in
397	   ToolResult.statistics (compose already never reads exploratory results — assert it).
398	6. Tests: tests/polaris_graph/test_outline_toolkit.py — per-tool unit tests incl. fold-in
399	   id-collision assert, unit_convert round-trips, calculator AST rejection cases.
400	
401	T2 — BATTERY HARNESS + FIRST 12 CASES
402	7. NEW scripts/outline_battery.py (runner: case loader, ProcessPool, child-env isolation,
403	   flock retrieval slots, per-case kill, collector, ranked_failures.md writer,
404	   history.jsonl).
405	8. NEW tests/battery/cases/*.yaml — H01, H05, H07, H08, H09, H10, H11, H14, H18, H19, H20,
406	   H21 first (they exercise T1 tools + carry the W1 acceptance forward). Fixtures: fetch the
407	   real corpora ONCE on the box (fixture-builder mode `--record` in the runner), commit under
408	   tests/battery/fixtures/.
409	9. Run the wheel; fix to zero S0/S1 on these 12.
410	
411	T3 — PARSE + SPREADSHEET TOOLS + REMAINING CASES
412	10. Register parse_table (wrap tools/pdf_table_extractor.py + html table parse),
413	    read_spreadsheet (openpyxl), sql_over_tables (extend tools/evidence_database.py with
414	    ad-hoc table load), extract_entities, search_scholar + citation_lookup (wrap
415	    tools/openalex_client.py + Crossref retraction field), parse_pdf_layout (mineru hook).
416	11. Add cases H02, H03, H06, H12, H16 + holdout set H04, H13, H15, H17, H22. Wheel to zero
417	    S0/S1 non-holdout, then first LOCK GATE (holdout + fresh-judge read).
418	
419	T4 — MCP LAYER + FIGURE READ
420	12. NEW tools/mcp_tool_adapter.py + config/settings/outline_mcp_servers.yaml (seat
421	    PG_OUTLINE_MCP=0 default OFF; content_policy enforcement at the bridge; §8.4 teardown).
422	    First servers: a filesystem server (report artifacts) and a fetch server (redundant
423	    with fetch_url — used as the adapter's own integration test).
424	13. parse_figure (qwen-VL via OpenRouter, figure_estimated contract) + kg_ops (networkx over
425	    extract_entities). Add H17 to the active set.
426	14. Final wheel to lock: full battery + holdout clean + fresh-judge line-read + the standing
427	    W-series acceptance (loop fires on thin, silent on saturated) re-confirmed.
428	
429	Throughout: PG_OUTLINE_AGENT stays the master seat (default OFF, byte-identical legacy);
430	every new tool honors the shared wall (`retrieval_deadline_monotonic`, outline_agent.py:1010)
431	and the gap ledger contract; docs/fsr_build_plan.md gets a pointer line to this file.
432	
433	---
434	
435	## PART 5 — WHY THIS STAYS GENERAL (not overfit)
436	
437	1. Tools are domain-free primitives (fetch, parse, convert, compute, compare); zero domain
438	   templates. Domain knowledge enters only through evidence content and weights (§-1.3).
439	2. The battery spans 6 domains and asserts BEHAVIOR + published gold, never prose or tool
440	   order; paraphrase variants + holdout + case rotation + the no-case-id-leakage grep gate
441	   make memorized fixes structurally impossible to land.
442	3. The faithfulness engine is untouched and remains the only hard gate; every new content
443	   path (fetch_url, MCP, figures) is forced through the SAME fold-in seam and the SAME
444	   verified-compute lane, so capability growth can never widen the rendering surface.
445	4. Mechanical battery PASS/FAIL is a routing signal for the fix loop only (§-1.1.1 class A);
446	   every lock requires the independent fresh-judge context-level line-read (class B). A
447	   toolkit that games the assertions still cannot pass a human-grade read of its transcripts.

1	"""T1 rich toolkit for the agentic outliner (docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md PART 4, step 1+5).
2	
3	``register_outline_toolkit(registry, workspace, agent_model, deadline)`` wires the read-only /
4	deterministic primitives + the moat ``verified_compute`` onto the driver's existing
5	``ToolRegistry``. The driver (``OutlineAgent``) does not change shape when tools are added — every
6	tool speaks the standard ``execute(evidence_store, data_points, client, **kw) -> ToolResult``
7	contract.
8	
9	Faithfulness posture of this toolkit:
10	  * Every tool here is READ-ONLY over the workspace (search_corpus, get_evidence, list_baskets,
11	    coverage_audit, preview_section_evidence) or DETERMINISTIC-COMPUTE (calculator,
12	    verified_compute). None fetches network content — so none re-enters evidence and none can
13	    widen the render surface.
14	  * ``calculator`` is EXPLORATORY: a bare arithmetic result carries no evidence span, so it is
15	    planner-facing only (``renderable=False``). To RENDER a derived number the agent must use
16	    ``verified_compute``, which re-derives it through the verified ``[#calc:]`` lane
17	    (outline/verified_compute.py). This is the same two-lane discipline as execute_python.
18	"""
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import asyncio
22	import re
23	from typing import Any, Callable, Optional
24	
25	from src.polaris_graph.tools.tool_registry import ToolDefinition, ToolRegistry, ToolResult
26	
27	_WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z0-9]+")
28	
29	# The evidence text fields, richest first (mirrors _fold_in / tradeoff_modeler ordering).
30	_TEXT_FIELDS = (
31	    "direct_quote", "statement", "full_text", "content", "extracted_text",
32	    "fetched_body", "raw_content", "raw_text", "page_text", "source_text", "body", "text",
33	)
34	
35	
36	def _row_text(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
37	    return max(
38	        (str(row.get(k) or "") for k in _TEXT_FIELDS),
39	        key=len, default="",
40	    )
41	
42	
43	def _tokens(text: str) -> list[str]:
44	    return _WORD_RE.findall(text.lower())
45	
46	
47	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
48	# calculator — single deterministic arithmetic expression (EXPLORATORY lane)
49	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
50	async def _tool_calculator(
51	    workspace: Any, *, expression: str = "", **_ignored: Any,
52	) -> ToolResult:
53	    """Evaluate ONE whitelisted-AST arithmetic expression (reuses tradeoff_modeler's validator +
54	    interpreter). Deterministic, no LLM, no sandbox. EXPLORATORY: the result carries no evidence
55	    span, so it is planner-facing (``renderable=False``) — to RENDER a derived number use
56	    verified_compute."""
57	    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.tradeoff_modeler import (  # noqa: PLC0415
58	        _eval_formula, _formula_names,
59	    )
60	
61	    expr = str(expression or "").strip()
62	    if not expr:
63	        return ToolResult(
64	            success=False, tool_name="calculator",
65	            markdown="calculator requires an `expression`.", error="missing_expression",
66	        )
67	    ok, reason, refs = _formula_names(expr, set())
68	    if not ok:
69	        return ToolResult(
70	            success=False, tool_name="calculator",
71	            markdown=f"calculator rejected {expr!r}: {reason}", error=f"formula_invalid:{reason}",
72	        )
73	    if refs:
74	        return ToolResult(
75	            success=False, tool_name="calculator",
76	            markdown=f"calculator is constant-only; unknown names {sorted(refs)}",
77	            error="unbound_names",
78	        )
79	    try:
80	        value = _eval_formula(expr, {})
81	    except (ValueError, ZeroDivisionError, OverflowError) as exc:
82	        return ToolResult(
83	            success=False, tool_name="calculator",
84	            markdown=f"calculator failed on {expr!r}: {exc}", error=f"eval_error:{str(exc)[:80]}",
85	        )
86	    return ToolResult(
87	        success=True, tool_name="calculator",
88	        markdown=f"`{expr}` = {value} (EXPLORATORY — planner-facing; render via verified_compute)",
89	        statistics={"expression": expr, "value": value},
90	        insights=[f"{expr} = {value}"],
91	    )
92	
93	
94	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
95	# get_evidence — full text + metadata of ONE evidence row
96	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
97	async def _tool_get_evidence(
98	    workspace: Any, *, ev_id: str = "", max_chars: int = 4000, **_ignored: Any,
99	) -> ToolResult:
100	    """Return the FULL text + key metadata of one evidence row by id (the agent normally sees
101	    only the digest). Read-only, no network."""
102	    eid = str(ev_id or "").strip()
103	    row = workspace.ev_store.get(eid) if eid else None
104	    if not isinstance(row, dict):
105	        return ToolResult(
106	            success=False, tool_name="get_evidence",
107	            markdown=f"No evidence row {eid!r} in the pool.", error="ev_not_found",
108	        )
109	    text = _row_text(row)
110	    try:
111	        cap = int(max_chars)
112	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
113	        cap = 4000
114	    body = text[:cap]
115	    md = [
116	        f"**{eid}** [CITE:{eid}]",
117	        f"- title: {str(row.get('title') or row.get('source_title') or '')[:200]}",
118	        f"- url: {str(row.get('source_url') or row.get('url') or '')[:200]}",
119	        f"- text ({len(text)} chars{', truncated' if len(text) > cap else ''}):",
120	        body,
121	    ]
122	    return ToolResult(

128	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
129	# search_corpus — keyword search over the CURRENT ev_store (read-only, no network)
130	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
131	async def _tool_search_corpus(
132	    workspace: Any, *, query: str = "", top_k: int = 8, **_ignored: Any,
133	) -> ToolResult:
134	    """Rank rows already in the pool by query-term frequency; snippet per hit. The mandatory
135	    'do we already have this?' stop BEFORE any live fetch. Read-only, no network, no LLM."""
136	    q = str(query or "").strip()
137	    if not q:
138	        return ToolResult(
139	            success=False, tool_name="search_corpus",
140	            markdown="search_corpus requires a `query`.", error="missing_query",
141	        )
142	    try:
143	        k = max(1, int(top_k))
144	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
145	        k = 8
146	    q_tokens = set(_tokens(q))
147	    if not q_tokens:
148	        return ToolResult(
149	            success=False, tool_name="search_corpus",
150	            markdown="query had no searchable tokens.", error="empty_query_tokens",
151	        )
152	    scored: list[tuple[float, str, dict]] = []
153	    for eid, row in workspace.ev_store.items():
154	        if not isinstance(row, dict):
155	            continue
156	        text = f"{row.get('title') or ''} {_row_text(row)}"
157	        toks = _tokens(text)
158	        if not toks:
159	            continue
160	        counts = sum(toks.count(t) for t in q_tokens)
161	        if counts <= 0:
162	            continue
163	        # length-normalized term frequency (cheap BM25-ish) so a long junk page can't win on raw counts
164	        score = counts / (1.0 + 0.001 * len(toks))
165	        scored.append((score, str(eid), row))
166	    scored.sort(key=lambda t: (-t[0], t[1]))
167	    hits = scored[:k]
168	    if not hits:
169	        return ToolResult(
170	            success=True, tool_name="search_corpus",
171	            markdown=f"No rows in the pool match {q!r} (searched {len(workspace.ev_store)}).",
172	            statistics={"matches": 0, "searched": len(workspace.ev_store)},
173	        )
174	    lines = [f"**search_corpus {q!r}: {len(hits)} of {len(scored)} matches**", ""]
175	    for score, eid, row in hits:
176	        snippet = _snippet(_row_text(row), q_tokens)
177	        lines.append(f"- {eid} (score={score:.2f}): {snippet} [CITE:{eid}]")
178	    return ToolResult(
179	        success=True, tool_name="search_corpus", markdown="\n".join(lines),
180	        source_evidence_ids=[eid for _, eid, _ in hits],
181	        statistics={"matches": len(scored), "searched": len(workspace.ev_store)},
182	    )
183	
184	
185	def _snippet(text: str, q_tokens: set[str], width: int = 160) -> str:
186	    low = text.lower()
187	    pos = -1
188	    for t in q_tokens:
189	        i = low.find(t)
190	        if i >= 0 and (pos < 0 or i < pos):
191	            pos = i
192	    if pos < 0:
193	        return text[:width].strip()
194	    start = max(0, pos - width // 4)
195	    return ("..." if start > 0 else "") + text[start:start + width].strip()
196

203	) -> ToolResult:
204	    """One line per basket: id, member count, work-level corroboration, assignment status. Lets
205	    the agent scan the whole corpus without N inspect_basket calls. Read-only."""
206	    menu = workspace.basket_menu
207	    member_map = dict(getattr(menu, "basket_member_ev_ids", {}) or {}) if menu is not None else {}
208	    if not member_map:
209	        return ToolResult(

211	            markdown="No basket digest available (no multi-member clusters).",
212	            statistics={"baskets": 0},
213	        )
214	    corr_map = dict(getattr(menu, "basket_work_corroboration", {}) or {})
215	    assigned_ev = _assigned_ev_ids(workspace)

226	        )
227	        lines.append(
228	            f"- {bid}: {len(members)} member(s), corroboration={corr_map.get(bid, len(members))}, {status}"
229	        )

237	# coverage_audit — deterministic (never-LLM) coverage accounting
238	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
239	async def _tool_coverage_audit(workspace: Any, **_ignored: Any) -> ToolResult:
240	    """Deterministic accounting: unassigned basket ids, per-section basket/ev counts, residual
241	    fraction. The O2/O3 math on demand so the agent SEES its own coverage. Never-LLM."""
242	    from src.polaris_graph.outline.outline_agent import _plan_field  # noqa: PLC0415
243	
244	    menu = workspace.basket_menu
245	    member_map = dict(getattr(menu, "basket_member_ev_ids", {}) or {}) if menu is not None else {}
246	    assigned_ev = _assigned_ev_ids(workspace)
247	
248	    unassigned_baskets = sorted(
249	        bid for bid, members in member_map.items()
250	        if not any(m in assigned_ev for m in (members or []))
251	    )
252	    total_ev = len(workspace.ev_store)
253	    n_assigned_ev = len(assigned_ev & set(workspace.ev_store.keys()))
254	    residual = 0.0 if total_ev == 0 else round(1.0 - n_assigned_ev / total_ev, 4)
255	
256	    per_section = []
257	    for p in workspace.outline_draft:
258	        title = str(_plan_field(p, "title", "") or "")
259	        ev_ids = list(_plan_field(p, "ev_ids", []) or [])
260	        basket_ids = list(_plan_field(p, "basket_ids", []) or [])
261	        per_section.append((title, len(ev_ids), len(basket_ids)))
262	
263	    lines = [
264	        f"**coverage_audit**: {total_ev} ev rows, {n_assigned_ev} assigned, "
265	        f"residual={residual:.2%}",
266	        f"- unassigned baskets ({len(unassigned_baskets)}): {unassigned_baskets[:40]}",
267	        "- per section (title: ev_ids, baskets):",
268	    ]
269	    for title, n_ev, n_b in per_section:
270	        floor_flag = " [BELOW FLOOR: 0 baskets]" if n_b == 0 else ""
271	        lines.append(f"    {title!r}: {n_ev} ev, {n_b} baskets{floor_flag}")
272	    return ToolResult(
273	        success=True, tool_name="coverage_audit", markdown="\n".join(lines),
274	        statistics={
275	            "total_ev": total_ev, "assigned_ev": n_assigned_ev, "residual": residual,
276	            "unassigned_baskets": len(unassigned_baskets),
277	            "sections": len(per_section),
278	            "sections_below_floor": sum(1 for _, _, n_b in per_section if n_b == 0),
279	        },
280	    )

325	# verified_compute — THE moat tool: derive a number that renders via [#calc:]
326	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
327	async def _tool_verified_compute(
328	    workspace: Any, *, question: str = "", datapoints: Any = None, spec: Any = None,
329	    render_field: Optional[str] = None, lead: str = "", section: str = "", **_ignored: Any,
330	) -> ToolResult:
331	    """Derive a number through the VERIFIED lane (build_quantified_spec -> execute_quantified_model)
332	    and return a render-eligible sentence carrying its ``[#calc:]`` token — the ONLY compute output
333	    allowed to render. Fail-closed: a bad spec yields success=False and renders nothing.
334	
335	    ``section`` names the outline section this number belongs in; the render-ready sentence is
336	    recorded on the workspace so the FULL-CORPUS composer can APPEND it into that section body
337	    DETERMINISTICALLY (the writer LLM cannot copy the unguessable spec_hash). If ``section`` is
338	    omitted the composer auto-homes the claim by matching the sourced datapoints' ev_ids against
339	    the section's ev_ids. Either way the appended sentence only survives strict_verify if the
340	    computed model verifies its token — emission is fail-closed."""
341	    from src.polaris_graph.outline.verified_compute import run_verified_compute  # noqa: PLC0415
342	
343	    if not isinstance(datapoints, list) or not isinstance(spec, dict):
344	        return ToolResult(
345	            success=False, tool_name="verified_compute",
346	            markdown="verified_compute requires `datapoints` (list) and `spec` (dict).",
347	            error="bad_args",
348	        )
349	    claim = await run_verified_compute(
350	        workspace, question=str(question or workspace.research_question),
351	        datapoints=datapoints, raw_spec=spec, render_field=render_field,
352	    )
353	    if claim is None:
354	        return ToolResult(
355	            success=False, tool_name="verified_compute",
356	            markdown="verified_compute: spec failed to build/execute (fail-closed) — no number rendered.",
357	            error="spec_rejected_or_exec_failed",
358	        )
359	    lead_text = str(lead or "").strip() or "The computed value is"
360	    sentence = claim.render_sentence(lead_text)
361	    # MOAT DETERMINISTIC EMISSION: record the render-ready sentence on the workspace so the
362	    # composer can inject it into the target section body verbatim (fail-closed: it renders only if
363	    # strict_verify re-verifies its [#calc:] token against the registered model).
364	    input_ev_ids = [
365	        str(dp.get("evidence_id"))
366	        for dp in datapoints
367	        if isinstance(dp, dict) and dp.get("evidence_id")
368	    ]
369	    claims_sink = getattr(workspace, "computed_claims", None)
370	    if isinstance(claims_sink, list):
371	        claims_sink.append({
372	            "section": str(section or "").strip(),
373	            "sentence": sentence,
374	            "calc_token": claim.calc_token,
375	            "input_ev_ids": input_ev_ids,
376	        })
377	    return ToolResult(
378	        success=True, tool_name="verified_compute",
379	        markdown=(
380	            f"VERIFIED computed value **{claim.display_value}** renders via `{claim.calc_token}`.\n\n"
381	            f"Render-ready sentence: {sentence}"
382	        ),
383	        statistics={
384	            "display_value": claim.display_value, "model_id": claim.model_id,
385	            "spec_hash": claim.spec_hash, "field_id": claim.field_id,
386	            "calc_token": claim.calc_token, "formula": claim.formula,
387	            "render_sentence": sentence,
388	        },
389	    )

395	async def _default_fetch_row(url: str, max_chars: int) -> Optional[dict[str, Any]]:
396	    """Production single-URL fetch: the AccessBypass cascade (Crawl4AI/Jina/Firecrawl + fallbacks)
397	    via live_retriever._fetch_content, shaped into ONE cp-format evidence row. Network path —
398	    exercised only in live runs; unit tests inject a fake fetcher instead."""
399	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.live_retriever import _fetch_content  # noqa: PLC0415
400	
401	    content, ok, title, _body_type, _jsonld = await asyncio.to_thread(
402	        _fetch_content, url, max_chars,
403	    )

412	async def _tool_fetch_url(
413	    workspace: Any, agent_model: str, *, url: str = "", max_chars: int = 20000,
414	    fetch_fn: Optional[Callable[[str, int], "Any"]] = None, **_ignored: Any,
415	) -> ToolResult:
416	    """Targeted re-fetch of ONE named URL (the agent saw a citation/link inside evidence and wants
417	    the primary). The fetched page re-enters evidence through the SAME shared fold-in seam as
418	    search_more_evidence (``fold_in_fetched_rows``: url-dedup -> offset-renumber with hard
419	    id-collision assert -> S2 stamp/delete -> insert), so a second content path can NEVER diverge
420	    from the id guard or the junk/off-topic screen. No number and no citation is invented here.
421	
422	    ``fetch_fn(url, max_chars) -> row|None`` is injectable so the fold-in re-entry is unit-testable
423	    without network; it defaults to the production AccessBypass cascade."""
424	    from src.polaris_graph.outline._fold_in import fold_in_fetched_rows  # noqa: PLC0415
425	
426	    u = str(url or "").strip()
427	    if not u:
428	        return ToolResult(success=False, tool_name="fetch_url",
429	                          markdown="fetch_url requires a `url`.", error="missing_url")
430	    try:
431	        cap = max(1000, int(max_chars))
432	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
433	        cap = 20000
434	    if u in workspace.existing_urls():
435	        return ToolResult(
436	            success=False, tool_name="fetch_url",
437	            markdown=f"URL already in the pool (no re-fetch): {u}", error="url_already_present")
438	
439	    fetcher = fetch_fn or _default_fetch_row
440	    try:
441	        maybe = fetcher(u, cap)
442	        row = await maybe if asyncio.iscoroutine(maybe) else maybe
443	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a single fetch must not crash the loop
444	        return ToolResult(success=False, tool_name="fetch_url",
445	                          markdown=f"fetch_url failed for {u}: {exc}", error=str(exc)[:300])
446	    if not isinstance(row, dict):
447	        return ToolResult(success=False, tool_name="fetch_url",
448	                          markdown=f"fetch_url got no usable content from {u}.", error="empty_fetch")
449	
450	    fold = await fold_in_fetched_rows(
451	        workspace, [row], research_question=workspace.research_question, agent_model=agent_model,
452	    )
453	    disclosure = (
454	        f"fetch_url[{u}] kept {fold.n_kept}, url-dup {fold.n_url_dup}, deleted {fold.n_deleted} "
455	        f"(chrome={len(fold.deleted_chrome)}, off-topic={len(fold.deleted_offtopic)})"
456	    )
457	    workspace.disclose(disclosure)
458	    kept_ids = [r.get("evidence_id") for r in fold.kept_rows]
459	    md = [f"**{disclosure}**"]
460	    for eid in kept_ids:
461	        md.append(f"- {eid}: {u} [CITE:{eid}]")
462	    return ToolResult(
463	        success=fold.n_kept > 0, tool_name="fetch_url", markdown="\n".join(md),
464	        source_evidence_ids=[e for e in kept_ids if e],
465	        statistics={"kept": fold.n_kept, "url_dup": fold.n_url_dup, "deleted": fold.n_deleted},
466	    )

469	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
470	# find_contradictions — surface conflict (direction / magnitude outlier), never average
471	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
472	_NUM_RE = re.compile(r"[-+]?\d[\d,]*(?:\.\d+)?")
473	
474	
475	def _headline_numbers(text: str) -> list[float]:
476	    """All parseable magnitudes in a row's text (commas stripped), skipping bare 4-digit years."""
477	    vals: list[float] = []
478	    for m in _NUM_RE.findall(text or ""):
479	        raw = m.replace(",", "")
480	        try:
481	            v = float(raw)
482	        except ValueError:
483	            continue
484	        # skip a bare calendar year (1900-2099) so a date is not mistaken for a magnitude
485	        if raw.isdigit() and 1900 <= v <= 2099 and "." not in raw:
486	            continue
487	        vals.append(v)
488	    return vals
489

491	async def _tool_find_contradictions(
492	    workspace: Any, *, ev_ids: Any = None, outlier_ratio: float = 100.0, **_ignored: Any,
493	) -> ToolResult:
494	    """Surface CONFLICT among a set of evidence rows on the same aspect — deterministic, spend-free.
495	
496	    Two conflict modes (design Category D #26): (1) DIRECTION conflict — one row asserts an explicit
497	    increase and another an explicit decrease on the endpoint; (2) MAGNITUDE outlier — one row's
498	    headline value is >= ``outlier_ratio`` x another's (the 1000x-off poison outlier, H22). Conflicts
499	    are surfaced as named material with BOTH ev_ids; nothing is averaged and nothing is deleted
500	    (§-1.3 weight, don't filter). Read-only: never mutates ``ev_store``.
501	
502	    NOTE: this is the deterministic core. Pairwise NLI-entailment via the mirror model is an
503	    OPTIONAL fail-open enrichment (not run here to stay spend-free); it can only ADD advisory
504	    conflicts, never suppress a deterministic one.
505	    """
506	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.contradiction_detector import _extract_direction  # noqa: PLC0415
507	
508	    ids = [str(e) for e in (ev_ids or list(workspace.ev_store.keys()))]
509	    rows = [(e, workspace.ev_store.get(e)) for e in ids]
510	    rows = [(e, r) for e, r in rows if isinstance(r, dict)]
511	    try:
512	        ratio = float(outlier_ratio)
513	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
514	        ratio = 100.0
515	
516	    raw_feats = []
517	    for e, r in rows:
518	        text = _row_text(r)
519	        raw_feats.append((e, _extract_direction(text), _headline_numbers(text)))
520	
521	    # Strip magnitudes that appear IDENTICALLY in every row (a shared denominator/unit such as the
522	    # "per 100,000" in a rate, or a common cohort size) — they are not distinguishing values and
523	    # would false-flag a magnitude outlier (rate vs its own denominator). Deterministic, order-free.
524	    shared: set[float] | None = None
525	    for _e, _d, nums in raw_feats:
526	        s = set(nums)
527	        shared = s if shared is None else (shared & s)
528	    shared = shared or set()
529	    feats = [
530	        (e, d, [n for n in nums if n not in shared] or list(nums))
531	        for e, d, nums in raw_feats
532	    ]
533	
534	    pairs: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
535	    involved: set[str] = set()
536	    for i in range(len(feats)):
537	        for j in range(i + 1, len(feats)):
538	            ei, di, ni = feats[i]
539	            ej, dj, nj = feats[j]
540	            if di and dj and di != dj:
541	                pairs.append({"a": ei, "b": ej, "type": "direction_conflict",
542	                              "direction": f"{ei}:{di} vs {ej}:{dj}"})
543	                involved.update((ei, ej))
544	                continue
545	            if ni and nj:
546	                hi, lo = max(ni), min(nj)
547	                hi2, lo2 = max(nj), min(ni)
548	                best = max(
549	                    (hi / lo if lo else float("inf")),
550	                    (hi2 / lo2 if lo2 else float("inf")),
551	                )
552	                if best >= ratio:
553	                    pairs.append({"a": ei, "b": ej, "type": "magnitude_outlier",
554	                                  "ratio": round(best, 2)})
555	                    involved.update((ei, ej))
556	
557	    if not pairs:
558	        return ToolResult(
559	            success=True, tool_name="find_contradictions",
560	            markdown=f"No direction/magnitude conflict among {len(rows)} row(s) "
561	                     "(agreement or unresolved — never silently averaged).",
562	            statistics={"conflicts": 0, "rows": len(rows), "pairs": []},
563	        )
564	    lines = [f"**find_contradictions: {len(pairs)} conflict pair(s)** across {len(rows)} rows", ""]
565	    for p in pairs:
566	        if p["type"] == "direction_conflict":
567	            lines.append(f"- DIRECTION CONFLICT {p['a']} <-> {p['b']}: {p['direction']} "
568	                         f"[CITE:{p['a']}] [CITE:{p['b']}]")
569	        else:
570	            lines.append(f"- MAGNITUDE OUTLIER {p['a']} <-> {p['b']}: {p['ratio']}x apart "
571	                         f"[CITE:{p['a']}] [CITE:{p['b']}]")
572	    lines.append("")
573	    lines.append("These are CONFLICTING-evidence material (surface both sides; never average, "
574	                 "never delete — §-1.3 weight, don't filter).")
575	    return ToolResult(
576	        success=True, tool_name="find_contradictions", markdown="\n".join(lines),
577	        source_evidence_ids=sorted(involved),
578	        statistics={"conflicts": len(pairs), "rows": len(rows), "pairs": pairs},
579	    )

594	def register_outline_toolkit(
595	    registry: ToolRegistry,
596	    workspace: Any,
597	    agent_model: str,
598	    deadline: Optional[float] = None,
599	) -> list[str]:
600	    """Wire the T1 read-only/deterministic tools + verified_compute onto ``registry``. Returns the
601	    list of registered tool names. The driver iterates the registry unchanged."""
602	
603	    def _bind(fn):
604	        async def _exec(evidence_store, data_points, client, **kw):  # noqa: ANN001
605	            return await fn(workspace, **kw)
606	        return _exec
607	
608	    def _bind_am(fn):
609	        # fetch_url needs agent_model (for the fold-in topic judge); capture it in the closure so
610	        # the driver's uniform execute(...) contract is unchanged.
611	        async def _exec(evidence_store, data_points, client, **kw):  # noqa: ANN001
612	            return await fn(workspace, agent_model, **kw)
613	        return _exec
614	
615	    specs = [
616	        ("fetch_url", _tool_fetch_url,
617	         "Targeted re-fetch of ONE named URL — turns 'source B cites source A' into actually "
618	         "reading A. Re-enters evidence through the SAME fold-in seam as search_more_evidence.",
619	         {"url": "the URL to fetch", "max_chars": "optional body cap (default 20000)"}),
620	        ("calculator", _tool_calculator,
621	         "Evaluate ONE arithmetic expression (deterministic, no LLM). EXPLORATORY — render a "
622	         "derived number via verified_compute, not this.",
623	         {"expression": "a pure arithmetic expression, e.g. (1493602-903095)*1000"}),
624	        ("get_evidence", _tool_get_evidence,
625	         "Return the FULL text + metadata of one evidence row by id.",
626	         {"ev_id": "the evidence id", "max_chars": "optional text cap (default 4000)"}),
627	        ("search_corpus", _tool_search_corpus,
628	         "Keyword search over the evidence ALREADY in the pool (no network). The 'do we already "
629	         "have this?' stop before any live fetch.",
630	         {"query": "keywords to search", "top_k": "max hits (default 8)"}),
631	        ("list_baskets", _tool_list_baskets,
632	         "One line per basket (id, members, corroboration, assignment) — scan the whole corpus "
633	         "shape at once.",
634	         {}),
635	        ("coverage_audit", _tool_coverage_audit,
636	         "Deterministic coverage accounting: unassigned baskets, per-section counts, residual "
637	         "fraction, sections below floor.",
638	         {}),
639	        ("preview_section_evidence", _tool_preview_section_evidence,
640	         "Preview one section's assigned evidence — exactly what compose will receive.",
641	         {"section": "the section title"}),
642	        ("find_contradictions", _tool_find_contradictions,
643	         "Surface CONFLICT among evidence rows (opposite direction, or a magnitude outlier) — both "
644	         "sides cited, never averaged, never deleted. Deterministic, no network, no LLM.",
645	         {"ev_ids": "list of evidence ids to compare (default: all in the pool)",
646	          "outlier_ratio": "magnitude-outlier threshold (default 100)"}),
647	        ("verified_compute", _tool_verified_compute,
648	         "Derive a number through the VERIFIED lane and render it via a [#calc:] token (the ONLY "
649	         "compute output allowed to render).",
650	         {"question": "what is being computed",
651	          "datapoints": "list of sourced datapoints {evidence_id,label,context,value,unit}",
652	          "spec": "the model spec {model_id,title,inputs,outputs}",
653	          "render_field": "optional output field to render (default first)",
654	          "lead": "optional lead prose for the rendered sentence",
655	          "section": "outline section title this number belongs in — the composer appends the "
656	                     "verified sentence into that section body deterministically (default: "
657	                     "auto-home by the sourced datapoints' ev_ids)"}),
658	    ]
659	    _agent_model_tools = {"fetch_url"}
660	    registered: list[str] = []
661	    for name, fn, desc, params in specs:
662	        binder = _bind_am if name in _agent_model_tools else _bind
663	        registry.register(ToolDefinition(
664	            name=name, description=desc, requires_data=False, requires_llm=False,
665	            parameters=params, execute=binder(fn),
666	        ))
667	        registered.append(name)
668	    return registered
